Title: Transcriptional Reprogramming Deploys a Compartmentalized ‘Timebomb’ in Catharanthus roseus to Fend Off Chewing Herbivores

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: anti-herbivory chemical defense as precursor component of toxic vindoline-catharanthine dimerization product
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Marker of herbivory response; induced in damaged and undamaged paired leaves after Manduca sexta feeding but not shown as causal regulator of vindoline accumulation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in locally damaged leaves and undamaged paired leaves after herbivory at both normal and low temperature
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Manduca sexta herbivory; normal temperature; low temperature (4°C)
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IPRT1 served as a validated herbivory-responsive marker gene. 2. Its expression increased in both damaged and systemic paired leaves after M. sexta feeding at NT and LT. 3. It was used to confirm herbivory signaling context for vindoline pathway regulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core common gene because it is experimentally measured in Results, but it is not a direct vindoline biosynthetic enzyme or decisive regulator.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: anti-herbivory chemical defense as precursor component of toxic TIA dimer; also tied to stress-priority switching
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold-induced CrSnRK2.7 represses CrMYBR2 and vindoline pathway gene expression while promoting CrCBF1 expression, shifting metabolism from TIA defense to cold tolerance
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript increased by ~50% after 24 h low temperature; overexpression under low temperature represses CrMYBR2 and vindoline pathway genes
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; yeast two-hybrid; luciferase complementation assay; overexpression in seedlings under low temperature; protoplast transactivation assay; RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrSnRK2.7 specifically interacts with HIVE, unlike CrSnRK2.6. 2. Its expression increases under low temperature. 3. CrSnRK2.7 enhances HIVE transactivation activity, represses CrMYBR2 and vindoline pathway genes under cold, and induces CrCBF1.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune tradeoff between anti-herbivory alkaloid production and cold tolerance; editing would require caution because it may suppress defensive alkaloids under cold
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced cold tolerance signaling may reduce vindoline/TIA accumulation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as an upstream signaling kinase rather than a TF or metabolic enzyme.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: vindoline pathway competes with cold-tolerance prioritization; CrCBF1 represents alternative stress-output rather than metabolite biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HIVE and CrSnRK2.7 induce CrCBF1 under low temperature, associating reduced vindoline production with increased cold tolerance
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Only CrCBF1, not CrCBF2/3, was induced by low temperature
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; overexpression; promoter transactivation assay; REL and MDA measurements
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrCBF1 is the cold-induced CBF-like gene in C. roseus. 2. HIVE activates CrCBF1 more strongly at LT than NT. 3. CrCBF1 overexpression improves cold tolerance, marking the stress-priority switch away from vindoline biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering cold tolerance, but likely at cost of lower vindoline-based defense allocation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Improved cold tolerance may coincide with reduced vindoline/TIA defense output
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a direct vindoline regulator in the herbivory branch; included because it is a core experimentally resolved node in the stress-reprogramming mechanism.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Terpenoid indole alkaloid biosynthesis; vindoline pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates tabersonine at C16 in the vindoline biosynthetic branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: component of anti-herbivory ‘timebomb’; combines with catharanthine to yield highly toxic dimerization product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is induced by herbivory in undamaged paired leaves and repressed by low temperature, tracking vindoline accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced under low temperature; induced in undamaged paired leaves after herbivory at normal temperature; repressed in HIVE- or CrMYBR2-silenced tissues and activated by their overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; transient overexpression of HIVE/CrMYBR2/BREVE; VIGS; promoter-GUS transactivation assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T16H2 is one of the key vindoline pathway genes transcriptionally induced by herbivory and suppressed by cold. 2. HIVE and CrMYBR2 positively regulate its expression, whereas BREVE represses it. 3. Its expression pattern closely parallels vindoline accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate structural gene for boosting vindoline accumulation, especially when combined with HIVE/CrMYBR2 activation and BREVE suppression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of four focal vindoline genes used for promoter and regulatory analyses.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Terpenoid indole alkaloid biosynthesis; vindoline pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a 3-oxygenation step in vindoline biosynthesis downstream of tabersonine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression rises with herbivory at NT and falls under low temperature, consistent with vindoline content changes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Decreased by low temperature; induced in undamaged paired leaves after herbivory at NT; activated by HIVE and CrMYBR2 and repressed by BREVE
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; transient overexpression; VIGS; promoter-GUS assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T3O is a cold-responsive and herbivory-responsive vindoline biosynthetic gene. 2. HIVE and CrMYBR2 upregulate its expression, whereas BREVE represses it. 3. Its transcript abundance correlates with vindoline accumulation changes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Structural-gene target for vindoline pathway enhancement in combination with upstream positive regulators
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains CR-box-related regulation but HIVE acts indirectly through CrMYBR2 for D4H; similar promoter activation pattern shown for T3O.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Terpenoid indole alkaloid biosynthesis; vindoline pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates deacetoxyvindoline to form deacetylvindoline in the late vindoline pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate late precursor to vindoline
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly activated by HIVE and CrMYBR2, repressed by BREVE, induced by herbivory at NT, and suppressed by cold; promoter used as central mechanistic reporter
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in undamaged paired leaves after herbivory at NT; repressed under low temperature; highly responsive to HIVE/CrMYBR2/BREVE manipulations
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; transient overexpression; VIGS; promoter deletion/mutagenesis; GUS assays; yeast one-hybrid for CrMYBR2-promoter binding
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. D4H is a principal reporter gene for the vindoline pathway regulatory circuit. 2. HIVE activates D4H indirectly through a CR-box-dependent CrMYBR2 route. 3. BREVE directly represses D4H through an E-box, and HIVE antagonizes this repression via heterodimerization with BREVE.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important late-pathway engineering target and useful promoter module for stress-responsive alkaloid pathway design
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: late-pathway control point, but rate-limiting status not formally established
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Most mechanistically dissected structural gene in the paper.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Terpenoid indole alkaloid biosynthesis; vindoline pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: component of anti-herbivory ‘timebomb’ that with catharanthine yields highly toxic dimerized product upon tissue disruption
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DAT expression is promoted by HIVE and CrMYBR2 and repressed by BREVE; transcript levels rise with herbivory and fall with cold, matching vindoline content
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced under low temperature; induced in undamaged paired leaves after herbivory at NT; responsive to HIVE/CrMYBR2/BREVE manipulations
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; transient overexpression; VIGS; promoter-GUS transactivation assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DAT is the terminal vindoline biosynthetic gene monitored in the HIVE-centered circuit. 2. Positive regulators HIVE and CrMYBR2 increase DAT expression, whereas BREVE represses it. 3. DAT transcript changes parallel vindoline accumulation in herbivory and cold treatments.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value terminal engineering target for increasing vindoline accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant for vindoline formation, but formal rate limitation not established
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Terminal biosynthetic step directly linked to vindoline output.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Terpenoid indole alkaloid biosynthesis; vindoline pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates a vindoline-pathway intermediate in the tabersonine-to-vindoline sequence
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: NMT expression decreases under low temperature, consistent with suppressed vindoline biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR time course under low temperature
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NMT is among the vindoline pathway genes suppressed by cold. 2. Its downregulation supports broad transcriptional repression of vindoline biosynthesis at low temperature. 3. It was not mechanistically dissected by promoter or TF assays in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible supporting structural-gene target for stabilizing vindoline production under non-cold conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Experimentally measured in Results but less central than T16H2, T3O, D4H and DAT.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: anti-herbivory defense as precursor component of toxic TIA ‘timebomb’; balances with cold tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates vindoline biosynthesis at normal temperature and after herbivory, but under cold its phosphorylation switches output toward cold tolerance and repression of vindoline pathway via CrMYBR2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master switch coordinating metabolic reprogramming between herbivory-induced vindoline production and cold-tolerance signaling
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: specialized metabolite biosynthesis and stress-priority switching
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CrMYBR2; T16H2; T3O; D4H; DAT; CrCBF1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates CrMYBR2 and, indirectly, vindoline pathway genes at NT; antagonizes BREVE repression; under cold/phosphorylation represses CrMYBR2 and activates CrCBF1
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation; indirect activation via secondary TF; condition-dependent specificity switch; sequestration/antagonism of repressor through heterodimerization
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Manduca sexta herbivory; low temperature; CrSnRK2.7-mediated phosphorylation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms homodimer; forms heterodimer with BREVE
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis; transient overexpression; VIGS; promoter-GUS assays; promoter truncation/mutagenesis; Y2H; luciferase complementation; phospho-mutant analysis; Phos-tag assay; immunoblot degradation assay; LC-MS/MS; RT-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Manduca sexta herbivory; low temperature (4°C); MG132 proteasome inhibitor
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in undamaged paired leaves after herbivory; unchanged in damaged leaves; unaffected transcriptionally by low temperature; protein phosphorylated and degraded in cold
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HIVE is a herbivory-induced subgroup IIIb bHLH that positively regulates vindoline accumulation by activating CrMYBR2 and vindoline pathway genes. 2. HIVE interacts with and antagonizes the repressor BREVE. 3. Under cold, CrSnRK2.7-dependent phosphorylation changes HIVE specificity: CrMYBR2 is repressed, CrCBF1 is activated, and prolonged cold causes HIVE degradation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering hub for conditional tuning of anti-herbivory alkaloids and cold tolerance; stacking with CrMYBR2 or phosphosite editing may enhance vindoline while preserving stress flexibility
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Boosting HIVE may divert resources toward TIAs at NT, whereas altered phosphoregulation could impair cold adaptation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Central regulator named Herbivore-Induced Vindoline-gene Expression.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates vindoline pathway genes and increases vindoline accumulation; acts downstream of HIVE
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct intermediate activator required for HIVE-mediated induction of vindoline biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates vindoline pathway genes, including direct CR-box-dependent activation of D4H
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Activated by HIVE; induced in undamaged paired leaves after herbivory; repressed by low temperature
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis; overexpression; VIGS; promoter-GUS assays; promoter mutagenesis; yeast one-hybrid; RT-qPCR; LC-MS/MS
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in undamaged paired leaves after herbivory; downregulated rapidly at low temperature; induced by HIVE overexpression and reduced by HIVE silencing
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrMYBR2 is the HIVE-responsive MYB-related activator linking HIVE to vindoline structural genes. 2. Overexpression increases vindoline and expression of T16H2, T3O, D4H and DAT, whereas silencing decreases them. 3. It directly activates D4H through a CR-box-containing promoter region.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising downstream activator for targeted vindoline enhancement with potentially more pathway specificity than HIVE
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could increase alkaloid allocation and associated metabolic cost
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CrMYBR1 was tested but not responsive to HIVE; CrMYBR2 is the functional intermediate factor.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses vindoline pathway gene expression and reduces vindoline accumulation; repression is antagonized by HIVE
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong direct repressor that sets a brake on vindoline biosynthesis and is neutralized by HIVE under permissive conditions
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses vindoline pathway promoters, including direct E-box-dependent repression of D4H
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression; repression antagonized by heterodimerization with HIVE
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Cold indirectly releases BREVE repression through HIVE degradation; BREVE transcript itself not induced by herbivory or low temperature
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms homodimer; forms heterodimer with HIVE
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression; VIGS; promoter-GUS assays; promoter mutagenesis; Y2H; luciferase complementation; RT-qPCR; LC-MS/MS
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal temperature; low temperature indirectly through HIVE degradation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript largely unaffected in damaged and undamaged paired leaves after herbivory and by low temperature
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BREVE is a subgroup Ia bHLH that represses vindoline biosynthetic genes and lowers vindoline accumulation. 2. Silencing BREVE elevates T16H2, T3O, D4H and DAT expression and increases vindoline. 3. HIVE binds BREVE and antagonizes its repression, but prolonged cold likely releases BREVE when HIVE is degraded.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Loss-of-function or promoter weakening of BREVE could raise vindoline levels; combination with HIVE/CrMYBR2 activation may be effective
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Removing repression may overcommit metabolism to specialized defense compounds
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named bHLH Repressor of Vindoline-gene Expression.
